summary: Comprehensive testing of Nagarik Operator Dashboard and Caregiver Dashboard. Backend: 31/31 tests passed (100%). Frontend: Both dashboards fully functional with all UI elements verified.

issues: React hydration warnings about span elements inside select/option - cosmetic only, caused by Emergent preview environment instrumentation, not affecting functionality

TestOperatorDashboardMetrics.test_metrics_admin_access: PASS - Returns active_sos, new_alerts, assigned, resolved_today, caregivers_online

TestOperatorIncidentsList.test_incidents_list_admin: PASS - Returns enriched list with senior_name, device_identifier

TestOperatorCaregivers.test_caregivers_list_admin: PASS - Returns caregivers with id, full_name, status

TestOperatorIncidentAssign.test_assign_incident_to_caregiver: PASS - Returns {assigned: true, incident_id, caregiver_id, assigned_at}

TestCaregiverProfile.test_profile_caregiver_access: PASS - Returns id, email, full_name, status

TestCaregiverAssignedUsers.test_assigned_users: PASS - Returns users with full_name, age, risk_status, active_incidents

TestCaregiverAlerts.test_alerts: PASS - Returns alerts with incident_type, severity, senior_name

TestCaregiverVisits.test_create_visit: PASS - Creates visit log with 201 status

TestCaregiverNotes.test_create_note: PASS - Creates health note with 201 status

operator_dashboard.header: PASS - data-testid='operator-header' with metrics (Active SOS: 20, New Alerts: 38, Assigned: 2, Resolved: 0, Caregivers: 2)
operator_dashboard.incident_queue: PASS - data-testid='incident-queue' shows 50 incidents with severity badges
operator_dashboard.filter_buttons: PASS - all, critical, open, assigned, resolved buttons work
operator_dashboard.incident_selection: PASS - Clicking incident shows dispatch panel
operator_dashboard.dispatch_panel: PASS - data-testid='dispatch-panel' shows incident details, assign buttons, resolve/escalate
operator_dashboard.assign_buttons: PASS - data-testid='assign-cg-{id}' for each caregiver (2 found)

caregiver_dashboard.header: PASS - data-testid='caregiver-header' shows Dr. Sarah Patel, Alerts: 1, Visits Today: 2
caregiver_dashboard.assigned_users_list: PASS - data-testid='assigned-users-list' shows 8 seniors with risk badges
caregiver_dashboard.user_filter_buttons: PASS - all, high, alert, not visited filters
caregiver_dashboard.alert_buttons: PASS - Start and Resolve buttons in alert cards
caregiver_dashboard.visit_form_fields: PASS - visit-senior-select, visit-purpose, visit-submit all present
caregiver_dashboard.note_form_fields: PASS - note-senior-select, note-type, note-severity, note-text, note-submit all present

critical_code_review_comments: OperatorDashboard.jsx: Well-structured with modular components (OperatorHeader, IncidentQueue, DispatchPanel, ActiveCaregivers, IncidentTimeline, QuickActions)
CaregiverDashboard.jsx: Clean separation with CaregiverHeader, AssignedUsersList, CaregiverAlertPanel, VisitLogPanel, HealthNotesPanel
All interactive elements have data-testid attributes for testing
RBAC properly implemented - caregiver blocked from /api/operator/*, operator blocked from /api/caregiver/*
Backend endpoints return enriched data (senior_name, device_identifier) for better UX
API uses async commit() properly after mutations
Frontend uses auto-refresh (15s for operator, 20s for caregiver) for real-time updates
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed visit log and health note during backend tests (not cleaned up - visible in caregiver dashboard)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Operator Dashboard at /operator-dashboard and Caregiver Dashboard at /caregiver both fully functional. Backend APIs: /api/operator/dashboard/metrics, /api/operator/incidents, /api/operator/dashboard/caregivers, /api/operator/incidents/{id}/assign, /api/operator/incidents/{id}/status, /api/operator/incidents/{id}/escalate. Caregiver APIs: /api/caregiver/profile, /api/caregiver/status, /api/caregiver/assigned-users, /api/caregiver/alerts, /api/caregiver/alerts/{id}/acknowledge, /api/caregiver/alerts/{id}/status, /api/caregiver/visits, /api/caregiver/notes. RBAC enforced - caregivers get 403 on operator endpoints and vice versa.
verified_features: GET /api/operator/incidents - returns enriched list with senior_name, device_identifier
GET /api/operator/dashboard/caregivers - returns caregiver list with status
GET /api/caregiver/profile - returns caregiver profile with status
GET /api/caregiver/assigned-users - returns seniors list with risk status
Frontend: /operator-dashboard with incident queue, map, dispatch panel, caregivers, timeline
Frontend: /caregiver with assigned users, alerts, visit log, health notes
